source_log: four team packets, tactics packets, rosters, player_state files

standings_inferred_from_local_round2_pos.context: draw likely enough for first place
win needed for first place; top-two position likely but lineup/rotation still material
draw protects third in group, win improves best-third comparison
must win to overtake DR Congo for third

group_context.same_time_dependency: Both K-group round-three matches should be treated as simultaneous; late-game risk appetite can change with the other match score.

top_concerns.concern: Small-sample overfit from Colombia two wins and Portugal 5-0
top_concerns.evidence: Available local inputs are round1/round2 predictions and postmatch reviews; no round3 model chain was found.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Recent results can be counted as form, player_state, tactics, and market signal at the same time.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Model should explicitly cap duplicate weighting from round2 results.

top_concerns.evidence: Colombia has six points; Portugal has four points and likely top-two safety.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: A draw-management script can raise the draw floor and lower open-play volume.

top_concerns.concern: Lineup, injury, suspension, and minutes gates are unresolved
top_concerns.evidence: No K round3 data packet or T-75 official lineup file was found.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Portugal rotation and Colombia key-player minutes can change both xG and tempo.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Refresh T-75 lineups, final injuries, yellow-card suspension, and minutes-risk table.

top_concerns.evidence: No same-source round3 1X2, handicap, and totals snapshot was found.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Portugal name plus 5-0 and Colombia two-win narrative can create opposite public premiums.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Collect official/same-source 1X2, handicap, totals, overround, and capture time.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: Colombia draw-management keeps the match low event and pushes 0-0 or 1-1 higher than a generic model would show.
Portugal scores first and Colombia's chase state creates a transition-heavy tail.
Colombia set pieces or late substitutes break a controlled Portugal possession script.
Early card, penalty, or red card invalidates a narrow Poisson score cluster.
overconfidence_flags: Do not carry Portugal 5-0 blowout tail directly into this stronger matchup.
Do not treat Colombia 1-0 over DR Congo as weak attack; shot volume and pressure mattered.
Do not use old player_state as proof of official round3 availability.
market_flags: Portugal public-team premium and 5-0 recency premium are live risks.
Colombia two-win/top-of-group narrative can also become overheated.
No same-source market chain means betting-ready language is blocked.
matches.recommended_probability_adjustment: Do not replace the model probability. Before official lineups, cap single-side certainty: if either team is above roughly 45%, require review; draw floor should remain in roughly the 27%-31% discussion band unless T-75 lineups and same-source odds justify otherwise.
matches.handoff_notes: Hold publication beyond discussion-only. Main thread must block all betting wording while verdict remains hold.

top_concerns.concern: Motivation is not equal to stable attacking quality
top_concerns.evidence: DR Congo can preserve third with a draw; Uzbekistan must win to overtake for third.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: One side may begin cautiously while the other only becomes fully open later.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Model separate first-60 and late-game incentive states.

top_concerns.evidence: Portugal postmatch review says early goals, set pieces, and errors amplified the scoreline.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: A single collapse should not erase Uzbekistan's transition and set-piece routes.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Apply blowout-tail learning conditionally rather than as a permanent team downgrade.

top_concerns.concern: DR Congo defensive signal may be goalkeeper-driven
top_concerns.evidence: Colombia postmatch review noted heavy shot pressure and goalkeeper high-save signal.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: Goalkeeper high points should not be translated into team defensive stability.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Separate goalkeeper form from defensive-block rating.

top_concerns.concern: Market and live availability gates are missing
top_concerns.evidence: No round3 official/same-source odds or T-75 lineups were found.
top_concerns.why_it_matters: This is a narrow third-place match where lineup and odds movement can swing the forecast.
top_concerns.follow_up_check: Refresh official lineups, final injuries, official card table, and same-source odds.

upset_or_failure_scenarios: Low-event draw if DR Congo protects third and Uzbekistan starts cautiously.
Uzbekistan scores first from set piece or second ball, flipping DR Congo into chase mode.
DR Congo transition win if Uzbekistan pushes fullbacks/wingbacks high after 60 minutes.
Early card, penalty, red card, or goalkeeper error creates a scoreline outside the central Poisson cluster.
overconfidence_flags: Do not price DR Congo as a stable favorite solely from Portugal 1-1 and Colombia 0-1.
Do not price Uzbekistan as noncompetitive solely from the Portugal 5-0 defeat.
Do not convert must-win motivation into full-strength attacking xG without lineup support.
market_flags: Any handicap wording must be translated into real-score conditions before use.
A possible DR Congo not-lose market signal would not equal a DR Congo win signal.
No same-source market chain means betting-ready language is blocked.
matches.recommended_probability_adjustment: Do not replace the model probability. Before official lineups, avoid any side above roughly 43%-45% without review; maintain a draw discussion band around 28%-32% and explicitly raise both first-goal and late-chase tails.
matches.handoff_notes: Hold publication beyond discussion-only. The match needs separate draw-protection, must-win chase, and collapse-tail branches.

data: Refresh group table, official tiebreakers, lineups, injury/suspension/minutes, weather/referee, and same-source odds.
tactics: Provide third-round matchup scripts with leading/trailing/draw-management branches.
model: Publish factor_inputs, weights, xG, Poisson, 1X2, totals, top scores, simulated group outcomes, and explicit anti-double-counting notes.
main: Keep output discussion_only; block all betting language while verdict is hold.
